entry.title: Questions for CDS aggregation expression
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a CDS view like header-item hierarchy, assuming 1 header to n items, and I want to do aggregation on item level.

I tried to group by on the header field and select the first item as aggregation result, but I only see expressions like MAX, MIN, AVG. Is there a way that I can simply use the line of first item.

The second question is that if I use MAX, how to handle with RAW type because it was not supported.

Last question is that if association exists, is it required to include all association fields in the group by field list?

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: New Member in the SAP Learning Community
entry.content:
Hey Everyone, 

My name is S'Maria Gray, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community!

I am currently a business development consultant that focuses on marketing strategy and UX research. I’ve enjoyed helping businesses create more efficient, user-centered systems and connect with their consumers to build community and communicate their business and product values. This experience has fueled my interest in how technology, like SAP, can be leveraged to optimize business processes and enhance strategic decision-making, particularly in understanding and serving user needs more effectively.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group. Being new to this arena, I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in customer experience (CX), marketing and supply chain has peaked my interest as well. These areas will allow me to improve how businesses interact with their customers, optimize their sales processes, and streamline operations.

I love learning new things and adding value to my tool belt. I believe this certification will help me grow professionally and provide new and impactful experiences that will help me bring meaningful improvements to organizations.

I’m open to connecting with others who are interested in customer experience, marketing, and supply chain and those who are finding innovative solutions to modern business challenges.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

SAP Marketing Cloud SAP Cloud for Customer integration with SAP Marketing SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain Customer Influence

entry.title: Making fields mandatory in a Personas flavor
entry.content:
One of the questions that is asked from time to time while working on Personas projects is how to make certain fields mandatory on screens. Usually there are multiple ways in the backend to do this, either via IMG configuration options, transaction / screen variants or application-specific features (for example the incompletion procedure in case of sales documents). If such a solution exists, it is generally best to fulfill the requirement using them. Another method could be some coding that takes advantage of a BAdI, enhancement spot or user exit. However, in some cases there may not be an easy way to solve the issue if none of the mentioned methods is available.

Let’s see how it is possible to achieve this goal with the use of an SAP Screen Personas flavor. I’m going to use the Create Sales Order (VA01) application as an example and want to make the “Sales office” field on the first screen a required entry.

The flavor editor offers a way to show a field seem as required. This visual option is available by setting the ‘Suggested’ attribute, after which the field will appear as required, displaying a red asterisk in the front.

Unfortunately, this will not actually enforce entering a value like a usual mandatory field. To do this, we must employ some scripting.

This check should happen after user interaction, like pressing Enter or selecting a menu option etc. Therefore, we need to perform this validation tied to the onBeforeRefresh screen event which is the last one before control is passed to the backend. Our goal is to interrupt processing if the “Sales office” field is left blank and issue an error message. However, there are some actions which we must not interrupt, because they are performing a standard navigation or user action.

We can use the following script snippet to do just that:

 

/* globals caller triggerType vkey */

switch(triggerType) {
    case session.findById("wnd[0]").EVENT_VCOMP:
        // If Back, Cancel or Exit pressed:
        if (caller && (caller === "wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[12]" || caller === "wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[15]" || caller === "wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[3]")) {
            // leave it to backend processing
            return;
        }
        break;
    case session.findById("wnd[0]").EVENT_VKEY:
        if ( vkey === 4 ) {
            // F4 Search Help
            return;
        }
        break;
}

 

This will first verify if the user selected one of the actions we do not want to interrupt and pass the control to the backend to continue as usual if either the Back, Cancel or Exit button is pressed or search help was requested.

In all other cases, our check logic should run to ensure that the “Sales office” field is populated. So, let’s add this to our script:

 

if (session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtVBAK-VKBUR").text == "") {
    session.findById("wnd[0]/sbar").setMessage("Fill out all required fields", "E");
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtVBAK-VKBUR").setFocus();	//set cursor to the empty required field
    return true;	//interrupt screen processing
}

 

Now if we assign this script to the onBeforeRefresh screen event, it will work exactly as we want, and it doesn’t let the user go to the next screen without specifying the “Sales office” value.

If we have multiple languages to deal with, we can certainly use a language-dependent error message instead of the hard coded one above, and use something like this to turn the text into a variable:

 

session.findById("wnd[0]/sbar").setMessage("$FLVRT:ER1", "E");

 

This variable is translatable via Flavor Administration to all languages we support.

In case we have more than one field on the screen that should be mandatory, we can optimize our script, so we don’t repeat the same logic for each. Let’s say, we also want the “Sales group” field to be mandatory, besides “Sales office”. So then, the second part of the script could look like the following:

 

if (isEmpty("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtVBAK-VKBUR") || isEmpty("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtVBAK-VKGRP")) {
    return true;	//interrupt screen processing
}

function isEmpty(fieldID) {
    if (session.findById(fieldID).text == "") {
        session.findById("wnd[0]/sbar").setMessage("Fill out all required fields", "E");
        session.findById(fieldID).setFocus();	//set cursor to the field
        return true;		//indicate an empty required field
    }
}

 

As mentioned earlier, the preferred way is to use backend features to make fields required, since that will also work for users who are not running a Personas flavor. This method is there if no such option is available. In addition, the above scripted solution can be useful if fields should be turned to be mandatory in a dynamic fashion. Like in this case, we could enforce entry in these fields only for certain order types.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunity you provide to individuals like myself who are seeking to improve their careers through SAP certification.

As a mid-career professional, I am eager to enhance my skills and knowledge in SAP, but I currently face financial constraints due to my family responsibilities. Unfortunately, I am unable to afford the certification fees at this time. Given the significance of this opportunity for my career switch, I would like to humbly request your support in offering me this wonderful opportunity .

entry.title: How to Restrict Material Determination
entry.content:
Hi 

we are using SAP Standard BAPI for sales order Change where we are substituting Material which have stock but in some cases Material determination is happening automatically in Run time , because in VA02 Material Determination is not happening when we enter manually.

Can anyone tell how to restrict Material determination in standard BAPI ???

 

Thanks in advance 

Harish Genikani

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content: (1 row affected) 1> Select top 1 * from SAPSR3.USR02 2> go Msg 10330, Level 14, State 1: Server 'AJD', Line 1: SELECT permission denied on object USR02, database AJD, owner SAPSR3 1> Select * from SAPSR3.USR02 where BNAME=’SAP*’ 2> go Msg 10330, Level 14, State 1: Server 'AJD', Line 1: SELECT permission denied on object USR02, database AJD, owner SAPSR3 1> use master 2> go 1> sp_db_name() 2> go Msg 102, Level 15, State 181: Server 'AJD', Line 1: Incorrect syntax near ')'. 1> select db_name() 2> go ------------------------------ master (1 row affected) 1> select db_name() 2> go ------------------------------ master (1 row affected) 1> use AJD 2> go 1> select db_name() 2> go ------------------------------ AJD (1 row affected) 1> select top 1 * from SAPSR3.USR02 2> go Msg 10330, Level 14, State 1: Server 'AJD', Line 1: SELECT permission denied on object USR02, database AJD, owner SAPSR3

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content: Hi Shareef, try to logon SIDADM or DB admin userid

entry.title: Re: is there any way to push comments from SAC to datasph...
entry.content:
Not sure if there is any standard option provided by SAP but you can try this workaround.

In SAC, use the OData service to push data to SAP Datasphere table via SAP BTP or SAP Integration Suite. You can expose your Datasphere table as an OData service and use SAC scripting to send comments via HTTP requests to the OData endpoint.

Another basic manual option- In SAC, Export to Excel/CSV to include comments linked to data points. Than use this Excel/CSV to load into Datasphere. 

Thanks

Savio

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 PDF Viewer Embedded not working on chrome
entry.content:
Hi Pedro,

Which version of Chrome do you use? I have v129 and can preview the PDF fine. You can raise a ticket here also, since it has nothing to do with your custom code.

https://github.com/SAP/openui5/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen

Regards,

Noël

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Hello All,

Here is my work set up 😊

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: Is it ok to use req._query[$filter] in a cus...
entry.content: Normally you don’t have to parse the query. You need to pass the query to the external service using the run command. Try following this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-app-ext-service-add-consumption..html

entry.title: Re: Document Change Rules, Line Item - Profit Center and...
entry.content: Hi @sumesh_gupta i agree with that, but my question is if change not allowed than why that fields available in change rule and what if client want to change or update business area and profit center for some transactions, because these effect every report which are not generated on company code level. Client want to update this for every transaction in which he doesn't entered these fields, what are the solutions for same except doing it via document splitting on new fiscal year which create clearing issues.

entry.title: Re: Map Invoice Time and Payment Time Tax Types and Tax C...
entry.content: Hi Vishal I am trying to system calculate WHT tax at the time of Invoice which already paid via advance. i have link the payment ref with invoice but system keep calculating WHT on same, but i have already paid the WHT on advance payment.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Brisbane #1
entry.content:
Join us for the very first SAP Stammtisch Brisbane.  Stammtisch is a German word meaning "regulars table" - referring to an informal group meeting held on a regular basis.  The group usually meets around a large round table which for us will take the form of a long bar table.  A Stammtisch is not a structure meeting but rather a friendly get together.

Let's put Brisbane on the map with our great community of SAPstars! at the very first Stammtisch Brisbane.  We look forward to see you there.

#SAPStammtischBRIS

entry.title: Re: Need to create PYSM story but with selection on month
entry.content:
Yes, I created restricted measure on date. "Restricted measure 1" will have system date or I can choose a date and I created another "restricted measure 2" with offset to PYSM.

But ,I need to be able to select date (Month) at the story level as a page filter and see PYSM data.

Is it possible?

entry.title: Re: can't log in NetWeaver AS ABAP trial
entry.content: Buradaki soru çözüldü ve farklı (SAP kullanıcısı engellendi), sorunuz Linux kullanıcısı oluşturulmamış (kurulum başarısız oldu), lütfen yeni bir soru açın.

entry.title: Re: Which one is the User permission tabel in SAP B1 data...
entry.content:
Hi mucipilbuga,

User permissions are stored in OUPT table.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Dynamic Agent (Parallel) Keeping Approver on Hold
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Need your help to build a code when requestor enter sales org, tax and finance it should be routed to all 3 approver ((Parallel) - finance / sales approver approved it and tax approver send for revision it, here it should go to requestor and requestor resubmit which is common requirement.

But here we want, When requestor resubmit it should only go to tax approver who send for revision not to finance/sales for approver because they already approved it and once tax approver approved it should trigger MERGE BADI (Parallel BADI). Somewhere we need to keep finance/sales approver keep on hold because it is already completed action and once tax approver approved it should trigger MERGE BADI (Parallel BADI).

entry.title: S/4HANA Output Management vs. NAST: When to Choose Which?
entry.content:
As SAP consultants, we often find ourselves standing at the crossroads of technology, gazing into the abyss of a decision. One road leads to the shiny, new world of S/4HANA Output Management with its fancy BRF+ engine 🚀, and the other? Well, it’s the path to the good ol’ reliable NAST, the grandpa of output management 👴. But just like choosing between a Tesla and your trusty 1998 Toyota Corolla, the decision isn't always as obvious as you might think. Recently, I was all aboard the S/4HANA Output Management train until it derailed spectacularly, forcing us to make a U-turn back to NAST. Buckle up, SAP adventurers, as we compare these two output options in the wild world of SAP and figure out when to embrace the future—or when to stay loyal to our old friend, NAST.

S/4HANA Output Management: The Bright, Shiny New Thing ✨

S/4HANA Output Management is the new kid on the block, powered by BRF+ (Business Rule Framework Plus). Think of BRF+ like that overachiever in school who could juggle five extracurricular activities while acing calculus. It dynamically handles outputs across processes like procure-to-pay and supports all the cool modern channels—email 📧, XML, EDI, and more—all with a slick Fiori interface. You’ll feel like you’re working in a futuristic office from a sci-fi movie.

Perks of S/4HANA Output Management 🎉

  1. Centralized Management 🗂️
    Imagine managing all your outputs from a single platform—like having a control panel for your spaceship! 🚀 You can streamline oversight and reduce miscommunication, all while feeling like a command center general. “Captain, we’ve got all systems go!”

  2. User-Friendly Fiori Interface 🖥️
    The Fiori interface is so intuitive, it’s like your tech-savvy friend giving you a guided tour of a new city—no maps or GPS needed! 🗺️ Say goodbye to navigating a maze of legacy systems. With Fiori, you’ll feel like you’re gliding through a theme park rather than wrestling with a labyrinth.

  3. Integrated Output Channels 📡
    S/4HANA Output Management comes with multi-channel support that integrates seamlessly with modern communication platforms—like the Swiss Army knife of output delivery! 🛠️ No need for extra tools; it’s ready to deliver real-time outputs faster than you can say “Where’s my coffee?”

  4. Scalability 📈
    As your business grows, this system scales up like a superhero putting on their cape! 🦸‍♂️ Need to accommodate increased output demands? No problem! S/4HANA Output Management flexes its muscles and handles it all without breaking a sweat (or a keyboard).

  5. Future-Proofing 🔮
    With the latest technology at your fingertips, you’ll be ready for whatever the future throws your way—like a cat with nine lives! 🐱 By leveraging cutting-edge solutions, you minimize the risk of obsolescence and keep your business relevant. Who needs a crystal ball when you have S/4HANA Output Management?

But before you get too excited, let's talk about where this whiz kid trips over its untied shoelaces.

Where S/4HANA Output Management Drops the Ball 🤦‍♂️

1. Limited Customization of Forms

You’re cruising along, all pumped up about your shiny new output management system, but then you need to add custom fields to your purchase order (PO). Uh-oh. S/4HANA Output Management is like a strict librarian—everything has to be just so 📚. You’re stuck with pre-delivered service nodes, and trying to sneak in your custom fields feels like asking for the Wi-Fi password in a no-tech zone. Sure, it’s possible with some effort, but you’ll need to break out the BRF+ manual (and maybe some aspirin).

NAST, on the other hand? It's like your cool uncle who lets you customize your ride any way you want. Want to add neon lights and custom rims (read: Smart Forms)? Go ahead! With NAST, you can easily enhance SAPscript or Smart Forms and flexibly bring in those custom fields without a lengthy approval process from the librarian.

2. Complex Extensions for Custom Output Formats

S/4HANA Output Management is great… until your client wants their invoices in a format that looks like it was designed by Picasso 🎨. Good luck extending those Adobe Forms. It’s like trying to add a spoiler to a spaceship—it’s not impossible, but it will take time and might defy physics.

NAST? It's the muscle car of output management: pop the hood, and you can tinker with everything 🔧. Whether you want your forms to look like fine art or just something a little more you, NAST lets you rev up those Smart Forms and SAPscript with ease.

3. Handling Multiple Outputs per Document

S/4HANA Output Management is like the one friend who gets flustered when you try to juggle multiple things 🤹. Need to send different versions of the same invoice to different people? Panic mode activated. Sure, BRF+ can handle it, but expect to be knee-deep in complex rules.

NAST, however, says, "Bring it on!" Want to send one invoice to the client, another to finance, and a third to yourself just because? NAST handles it like a pro, thanks to its easy condition records. No sweat 😅.

4. Flexibility in Condition-Based Output Determination

BRF+ tries its best with rule-based output determination, but sometimes it just doesn’t get the memo 📜. Extending BRF+ to accommodate weird, custom conditions can feel like trying to teach a cat to fetch. Possible, but expect resistance 🐱.

NAST, though? NAST is a dog—it loves fetch, and you can train it to do all sorts of tricks. Want outputs based on complex vendor categories, approval levels, or whether Mercury is in retrograde? NAST’s condition-based framework can handle it all with a wag of its tail 🐶.

Example: When S/4HANA Met Its Match

In one project, we thought we’d impress the client by using S/4HANA Output Management for their purchase orders. It was all smooth sailing until—BAM!—we hit the iceberg of "missing custom fields" ⚓. No amount of BRF+ tweaking could save us. We wasted days trying to bend it to our will, only to realize NAST would have done it in a fraction of the time. So, we jumped back into NAST’s arms, customized the PO with Smart Forms, and sailed off into the sunset 🌅.

S/4HANA Output Management vs. NAST: The Tale of the Tape

Feature S/4HANA Output Management NAST (The Classic)

TechnologyBRF+SAPscript / Smart Forms
Customization FlexibilityLimited by service nodesCustomizable to your heart’s content
Form TypeAdobe FormsSAPscript, Smart Forms
Output TypesPrint, Email, XML, EDIPrint, Email, Fax, EDI
Multiple Outputs per DocumentBRF+ (but expect headaches)Easier management with condition records
Learning CurveSteep—better grab some coffee ☕Familiar, like catching up with an old friend
Centralized ManagementYes—streamlined oversightTraditional, less streamlined
Future-ProofingYes—leverages the latest technologyNo—legacy technology means potential obsolescence
Recommended Use CaseStandard scenarios, new systemsCustom-heavy projects, legacy systems

Choosing the Right Output Path 🛤️

When to Choose S/4HANA Output Management:

  • You’re looking to impress with modern, scalable tech.
  • You want centralized management for oversight and efficiency.
  • Your output requirements are standard and predictable.
  • You like the user-friendly Fiori interface and want to leverage integrated output channels.

When to Choose NAST:

  • You have a quirky project with unique needs—custom fields, complex conditions, or multiple output formats.
  • You like the comfort of tried-and-true technology.
  • You enjoy the flexibility and ease of SAPscript and Smart Forms, like an old pair of comfy slippers 🥿.

Conclusion

S/4HANA Output Management is the sleek sports car of output systems—modern, fast, and looks great in the showroom 🚗. But sometimes, when you need to haul a weird-shaped load (read: custom fields and complex outputs), you’ll find that trusty NAST still has plenty of mileage left in the tank. So, whether you're cruising into the future or sticking with the classics, just remember: it’s not about the tech—it’s about what gets you to the finish line with fewer headaches 🎉.

entry.title: Applying flexibility changes in fiori elements with sapui5
entry.content:
How can I apply the flexibility changes settings stored in a file called changes-bundle.json in my changes folder which is generated when I build the MTA to deploy it in SAP BTP in the workzone and cloud foundry

Thanks for your support

This is the file

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content: The error message says "owner SAPSR3" so if you login with database user SAPSR3 it should work (isql -USAPSR3 -SNPL -X)

entry.title: Re: I am unable to log in with SAP* or DDIC user passwords
entry.content:
Hi @mmshareef 

For SAP*:

- login/no_automatic_user_sapstar must be set to 0

- there must be no sap* user record to use the default password - if there is you need to know the password assigned to the sap* user

Some useful links on the subject

Note 2383 - Documentation: description of "super user" SAP* 

Note 68048 - Deactivating the automatic SAP* user 

Activating Automatic sap* user 

Profile Parameters for Logon and Password (Login Parameters) 

 

I hope this helps

 

Gavin

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Visual Cloud Functions - "RBAC: access denied" on retrive, create
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a Visual cloud function project in SAP Build Apps and created an entity. When I tried to retrive the entity via Browser Data  or add a row manually via Browser Data I get the below error.

 

 

The role and user configuration looks ok to me. Tried deleting and reconfiguring the roles but did not help.

I made sure the App is deployed after changing the roles.

I am using the same email id to login. 

 

 

Any pointers on this is highly appreciated.
@Dan_Wroblewski @JoeBinkley @beapasch

entry.title: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : Unlocking the Power of AI in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
AI in Action

Artificial Intelligence is no longer just a buzzword—it’s reshaping how we work, especially in HR. SAP SuccessFactors continues to revolutionize HR technology with its AI-powered features in the 2H 2024 release. Artificial intelligence is seamlessly integrated to enhance productivity, personalization, and decision-making. Here’s a comprehensive look at the AI-driven functionalities that are making waves across multiple modules.

1. AI-Assisted Microsoft Teams Chat Integration

  • Module: Platform

SAP SuccessFactors introduces an AI-driven chat integration with Microsoft Teams, designed to streamline communication. The AI assists by providing contextually relevant responses and suggestions, making collaboration more efficient and user-friendly. With this feature, users can initiate quick actions and complete tasks simply by typing natural-language requests. The feature supports multiple locales and recognizes different phrases with the same intention. For example, whether users type "I want to request a day off" or "request OOO" or something else with the same meaning, the app will return the Request Time off quick action to help them proceed.

AI-Assisted Microsoft Teams App Chat, is now available in Work Tech

 

2. AI-Assisted Skill Recommendations from Continuous Performance Management

  • Module: Performance & Goals / Platform

Using data from Continuous Performance Management (CPM), Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities to infer and recommend skills to employees in their Growth Portfolio. This ensures employees are always aware of the skills they should focus on to enhance their growth and development.

Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities

Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities

 

3. AI-Assisted Skills Standardization

  • Module: Platform

We can now use AI-assisted capabilities to use the industry-standard skill names in the Attributes Library. Consistency is key, and SAP SuccessFactors addresses this by leveraging AI to standardize skill names across job profiles, resumes, and talent pools. This feature minimizes redundancy and ensures uniformity in skill tracking.

TIH Skills Standardization

 

4. AI-Assisted Career Insights

  • Module: Succession & Development

In Career Development, now leveraging generative AI capabilities, employees can gain insights into development actions they can take towards their target role. AI technology into the Career Worksheet feature, can unlock a wealth of knowledge and recommendations to accelerate their career development journey.

Boost your career with AI-powered insights

 

5. AI-Assisted Writing for Message to Recipient of a Spot Award

  • Module: Compensation

When sending messages for Spot Awards, AI assists managers by generating impactful message templates. This feature not only saves time but ensures that communications are professional and motivating. When you select the AI tool after selecting any text in the Message to Recipient text box, AI-assisted writing options other than the Generate Message are available to use.

AI-assisted writing features for reward recipient message

 

6. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing in 360 Reviews

  • Module: Performance & Goals

The 360-degree feedback process benefits from AI-driven writing assistance, enabling reviewers to provide structured and constructive comments. This is especially useful for providing comprehensive feedback with minimal effort. Two action items are now accessible from the context menu of that tool: Improve My Feedback & Generate Comments.

AI-assisted writing tool to help generate comments and improve feedback

 

7. AI-Assisted Insights into Detailed 360 Report

  • Module: Performance & Goals

The AI in 360 reviews didn't stop at writing— it also offers detailed insights into 360 review reports. By analyzing patterns and data, AI helps managers make better-informed decisions about performance and talent development. The generative AI in the Detailed 360 Report can be used in two scenarios: Overall understanding of a Detailed 360 Report & Consolidated analysis of a specific skill or competency.

Overall Insights for 360 Report

 

8. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing in Opportunity Marketplace Assignments

  • Module: Opportunity Marketplace

In Opportunity Marketplace, assignment owners and co-owners can now create and edit assignments using generative AI capabilities. This feature generates relevant assignments based on organizational needs, allowing for optimal talent allocation. When assignment owners or co-owners use the updated Enhance Writing action, it now considers the following context in addition to the existing description text:

  • Assignment type
  • Assignment title
  • Job role defined for the assignment when job role is enabled for assignments
  • Attributes added for the assignment

AI-Assisted Writing in OMP

 

9. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Goal Creation

  • Module: Performance & Goals / Succession & Development

As you know Goal Management module features an AI-assisted goal creation tool.SAP made several enhancements to AI-assisted goal creation in the latest Goal Management. Enhancements includes:

  • Updated AI tag : "AI-generated content", replacing the previous "AI-enhanced content"
  •  Streamlined content comparison: AI-assisted writing tool is now in place to enhance content directly at the field level. Compare Generated Content page has been removed.
  • Improved prompt template: prompt template is now optimized to automatically apply goal plan configurations, such as start, due, and maxlength, to AI-generated goals
  • Support for custom fields: Custom fields of text and textarea types are now supported for performance goals. By providing meaningful text for <field-description>, you specify the context, which in turn helps improve the quality of AI-generated content.

AI-Assisted Goal Creation

 

 

10. AI-Assisted Job Skills on Career Sites

  • Module: Recruiting

Recruiting has been enhanced with AI-driven skill matching on career sites. This feature helps highlight the most relevant skills for job postings, ensuring companies attract the right candidate. With this enhancement, you can now add a Skills Cloud component to the Job layout in Career Site Builder, so that job skills are displayed on your career site in the form of a word cloud.

AI-Assisted Skills Cloud

 

11. AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Candidate Resumes

  • Module: Recruiting

AI also plays a crucial role in resume screening by matching candidates' resumes with job requisitions. This speeds up the recruitment process and ensures that only the best-fit candidates move forward. When the AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes feature is enabled, the search results page on career sites displays the option to upload a resume. You have the option to present the candidate with a data privacy consent statement (DPCS) before they upload it. After they accept the DPCS and upload their resume, we use AI to analyze the resume and identify their skills. Then, we recommend jobs that match their skills.

AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes

 

12. AI-Assisted Skills Validation for Job Requisitions

  • Module: Recruiting

Before job postings go live, AI ensures that all required skills are validated. This prevents mismatches between job requirements and candidate qualifications, making the recruitment process more efficient. Recruiters can perform the following tasks to enhance the skills listed in the job requirements:

  • Add, remove, and save skills in the job requisition to control the visibility of the skills listed in the job posting on the career site.
  • Categorize primary and secondary skills to find candidates who best fit the job based on their skills matching the skills specified in the job requirements.

Skills Validation for Job Requisitions

 

13. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing Across Modules

  • Module: Across Suite

The Text Analyzer, a new AI-enhanced feature, brings advanced capabilities such as translating text and analyzing for biases. It scans for potential age, gender, and cultural biases, ensuring inclusivity and fairness in communications .

The following modules are adopting AI-assisted writing for the first time in the 2H 2024 release:

  • Compensation
  • Rewards and Recognition
  • Variable Pay
  • Calibration
  • Employee Central
  • Platform
  • Learning
  • Onboarding
  • Dynamic Teams (including Objectives and Key Results)
  • Latest People Profile
  • Talent Intelligence Hub

Text Analyzer scans for the following safety attributes like age bias, gender bias, etc.

AI-Assisted Writing & Translations

 

14. AI in Learning: Use AI to Associate Skills With Learning Items

  • Module: Learning

The Learning module benefits from AI, which associates relevant skills with learning content automatically. This ensures employees are always learning the right skills for their roles. You can use the AI-assisted capabilities to associate skills with Open Content Network (OCN) items based on the predefined skills provided by the OCN providers and thru imports for all learning items.

Learning Items - Skills Inference

 

 

This comprehensive list of AI features captures the essence of the 2H 2024 release and its significant impact on HR management. These innovations making sure that employees and managers can focus on what truly matters: growth, development, and success. AI isn’t just changing how we work—it’s making HR more human.

Are you ready to embrace the future of work?

Driving HR Innovation with Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: DUMP ISSUE
entry.content:
Hello dear community

I am facing this dump in production "CONVT_NO_NUMBER"

Category ABAP Programming Error
Runtime Errors CONVT_NO_NUMBER
Except. CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER
ABAP Program SAPLFMCA_BRFPLUS_CONTEXT_CONV
Application Component IS-PS-CA

Short Text
"NaN" cannot be interpreted as a number.

Any help for this issue?

Many thanks.

Mucip:)

entry.title: What is Composable Architecture?
entry.content:
So, what is Composable Business Architecture? Is this yet another "buzzword", or this is genuine approach to the Enterprise Architecture, Gartner is talking about[1]?

How is this related with Decoupled Integration (Architecture)?

And how is all this related with Event-Driven Architecture?

My 2 cents…

It’s a kind of buzzword, yes, not necessarily Gartner invented it – but it is meaningful one, to be honest….

Gartner talks about Composable Business (Architecture) in fact[1] – listing 4 main principles:

  • More speed through discovery
  • Greater agility through modularity
  • Better leadership through orchestration
  • Resilience through autonomy

If we go deeper – in the essence, it’s about (Architecture) building blocks supporting different business operations – which are connected seamlessly with each other. Of course, blocks are replaceable by other blocks (i.e. other products) supporting the same business operations or extending it. Actually, one may say nothing new that TOGAF is already not teaching us to do, as part of ADM[2]…

Sounds good? Well, maybe I am over-simplifying – but this is basically it...

We are talking about (Architecture) building blocks, which are not just built in any "best fit" style, but built in such a way to support agility, flexibility and resilience… And this is the main differentiator (and the main benefit, as well) of the modern Compossible Business (Architecture).

Clearly, Decoupled Integration (Architecture) is an enabling layer for the Composable Architecture. To put this in a very simple words (again), integrating via middleware and using API/Event Portal(s) is just must. What kind of "flexibility" would it be, if we would design P2P integrations between 2 building blocks? Probably none...

So, is Event-Driven Architecture answer to all?

Well, not really.

Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) is indeed one of the key Integration Styles providing Decoupled Integration. EDA, in its core, is built to be “decoupled”, but it is primarily executed asynchronously – although we are more and more talking about (near) real time integration as well[3] (I do strongly recommend this article in Solace from Bruno Baloi - pls note, when we talk about Solace PubSub+, we are also talking about SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh)

Fully synchronous integration, like REST or OData APIs can also be built through integration middleware (i.e. SAP Integration Suite incl. API Management) – let’s use Omni-channel integration as an example – where multiple commerce channels through the integration middleware are taking (in both synchronous and asynchronous fashion) orders on the same backend[4][5].

EDA is a key substance of the modern Decoupled Integration (Architecture) and thus of the Compossible Business (Architecture) – but we are not limited to EDA only… We should not abandon other good patterns[6] – as not one size fits all...

Final…

Those are my 2 cents…

Of course, would be glad to hear other thoughts as well…

In my next article(s) I will try to talk more about some practical examples (Events, APIs) and how those are addressing 4 principles of the modern Compossible Business.

Acknowledgment

*) Intro photo by Ryan Quintal on Unsplash

References

[1] The Future of Business Is Composable - Gartner Keynote

[2] Introduction to the ADM (opengroup.org)

[2] Why it's Time to Event Enable your Integration | Solace

[4] Building Integration Architecture for the Omni-cha... - SAP Community

[5] Part 3: Next steps in building the Integration Arc... - SAP Community

[6] Agile EA - from SOA to Interoperability - SAP Community

entry.content: My most favourite extensions.

entry.title: Re: Sap Webi Multivalue Error
entry.content:
- your screenshot does not start with the ID 🤔
- be sure that your ID is a dimension and not a measure
- be sure that you use the merged-ID-object in your table  
- try with min() or max() or first() or last() to avoid Multivalue
- start building your table column by column to find the problematic data and fix this column at first

entry.title: Re: continuous date range
entry.content:
you can try with min() or max() or first() or last() to find the requested dates
and you have to use the context of IN([Employee No]) in your formula to eliminate all other dimensions in your table

entry.title: Re: Can Azure AD be used in SAP Task Center config in pla...
entry.content: Im not an expert in entra id but you could connect it directly with BTP subaccount the same way the documentation describes it with ias: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/scenario-azure-first-sap-identity-integration . Instead of IAS you do this directly with your BTP subaccount. That should be the first step. Afterwords you should try to generate the global user id and provision it to your backend. Which provisioning tool do you use? This could be done with IPS.

entry.title: 🟠 Micro Frontends - What they are and how to implement them with Luigi
entry.content: In this talk, we’ll explore micro frontends - an architecture that breaks down large monolithic applications into smaller manageable pieces. Learn what micro frontends are and the benefits of using them across multiple teams with multiple tech preferences. There will also be a coding demo on getting started with Luigi and connecting several micro frontends.

entry.title: Re: What is Composable Architecture?
entry.content:
Hi Stevang , 

Thanks for sharing your insights on Composable Business Architecture! I agree that while it may seem like a buzzword, the principles from Gartner—speed, agility, leadership, and resilience—are crucial for modern enterprise architecture.

You’re right about Decoupled Integration being essential; using middleware and APIs enhances flexibility. EDA is indeed key, but it’s important to recognize that synchronous integration methods also play a vital role.

I look forward to your future articles on practical applications of these concepts!

Regards ,

Azmath.

entry.title: Re: New Member in the SAP Learning Community
entry.content:
Hi SMariaGray ,

Welcome to the SAP Learning Community! Your background in marketing and UX research will bring great insights as you explore SAP. I share your interest in customer experience, marketing, and supply chain, and I’d love to connect and exchange ideas!

Best of luck with your certification journey. Excited to learn and grow together!

Regards ,

Azmath.

I especially appreciate the emphasis on communication with partners to ensure a smooth transition. This will definitely help many in managing their SAP integrations effectively!

Regards ,

entry.content:

The step-by-step approach makes it easy to follow along, especially for those new to the SAP Cloud Integration environment. I appreciate the focus on Microsoft Entra ID for the "Client Credentials" flow. This will be a valuable resource for anyone looking to enhance their integration capabilities. I’m particularly interested in the potential use cases for the authorization code flow with IAS and XSUAA. Great job!

Regards,

I want to pursue the SAP Certified Certified Associate - Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA - SAP S/4HANA certification, as it aligns perfectly with my career aspirations in financial management and implementation. Participating in this program will provide me with valuable insights and the opportunity to learn from experts while connecting with like-minded individuals in the SAP community. I look forward to this exciting journey!

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities

entry.title: Hi Everyone
entry.content:
I have a strong interest in SAP due to its pivotal role in streamlining business processes and enhancing operational efficiency. My background in IT has equipped me with a solid foundation in systems analysis and exposure to software development, allowing me to appreciate the complexities and capabilities of enterprise resource planning.

Growing up in a remote area where technology is still advancing, I faced significant limitations in technology resources. This experience fueled my passion for leveraging technology to drive progress and innovation. I learned to adapt and find creative solutions with limited tools, which has strengthened my problem-solving skills and resourcefulness.

I believe my unique perspective, combined with my technical skills, makes me a good fit for this program. I am eager to deepen my understanding of SAP and contribute to projects that harness its power to make a meaningful impact, especially in under-resourced communities like my own. I am excited about the opportunity to learn from industry experts and collaborate with peers who share my passion for technology and its transformative potential.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite Deploy Undeploy Config tool Part 2
entry.content: Hi @fatima4 - Nice blog. Would have been nice if all the iFlow steps are explained and also modularly designed. Also we can use OData adapter and avoid passing CSRF tokens etc? What is the reason of using HTTP adapter? How much time it takes to undeploy 200 iFlows at a time? We can use a timer step and that gest trigered automatically based on undeply schedule and avoid manually invoking throug postman? Also Iterating splitter causes more overhead due to independent executions for each split. General splitter is preferred?

entry.title: Re: 🟢 The superpower of CAP CDS: Calculated Elements and Annotation Expressions
entry.content: Amazing session!!

entry.title: Create CDS Entity in system 2 based on table in system 1
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are looking to create a CDS entity in system 2 based on data present in system 1. This entity in system 2 will be used by PaPM tool in system 1 for further processing, based on the following requirement:

In system 1, PaPM team are trying to build a solution, but the source data is present in system 2. Source data is too huge, and filters applied via PaPM in system 1 aren't working effectively. We are looking to create CDS entity in system 2, based on the filters maintained in system 1 (which is an ABAP table), so that PaPM can use the CDS created in system 2 as a source with less number of fields. 

We tried the following options, but they don't fit our requirement:

1. Custom entity and ABAP interface (Custom entity can't be used for data fetch and can be used for RAP framework only, so couldn't go further)

2. Entity with Virtual data elements (Entity doesn't reflect the values calculated via Virtual data elements, unless used in Odata/RAP, which doesn't meet our requirement.)

Is there any way to achieve this? 

I see one way is to create the filter table in system 2, and create CDS in system, but this requires efforts in building an Fiori app based on RAP framework (for user input), Odata (for excel upload) and UI5 coding.

Thanks & Regards,

Naga

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: my vscode installations.

entry.title: Re: Error while posting MIRO Document.
entry.content:
Hi Kiran,

Since, our system configuration was perfectly alright. We had made changes to our "Message Control" Table, which solved our issue. And it was, as suggested by SAP.

SPRO Path : Logistics General -> Tax on Goods Movements - > India - > Tools -> Message Control

Change the Application Area : J_1IG_MSGS, Message No. 14 & 15, TCode: MIRO to "Warning".

Regards,

Chandan Barai

entry.title: Re: How to set Frame Options for Apps in Launchpad service?
entry.content: Have you tried whitelisting the url of the standalone approuter in the BTP subaccount where you have the Launchpad running? You can do this in the subaccount-> security-> settings

I believe I am a good fit for this program because I am not only eager to expand my skills but also enthusiastic about collaborating with others to create impactful digital solutions. entry.title: SAP and Arm64 version of Windows 11 with Prism emulator (Snapdragon X series)
entry.content:
I'm wondering if anyone has experience with the new Snapdragon X series Windows 11 PCs. My expectation is that all new cloud solutions should work seamlessly through the browser, just like on x64 or x86 systems. However, for other tools such as SAP GUI, Java GUI, ADT (ABAP Development Tools), and even the OpenJDK for Windows on ARM64, the situation may be different. I understand that it's still early days, and some older solutions may never receive full support.

I'm also interested in how well the Prism emulator works for running SAP applications. I’m presuming that even if emulation adds a bit more than 20% processing overhead, it wouldn’t be too significant or even noticeable if everything functions as expected. The challenge is with the drivers and VPNs, which do not work under the Prism emulator.

Despite these concerns, there is already a substantial list of native software for Windows on ARM that’s available today. It will be interesting to see how the ecosystem continues to develop.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
My dev-space. Ubuntu as main OS.

My favourite vs code extensions:

  • GitHub (copilot, actions, pull requests)
  • todo tree
  • dev containers
  • sqlite viewer

entry.title: Re: Preparing for Universal Parallel Accounting
entry.content:
This is very good detailed information and thanks for taking the time to put it together!  The note 3191636 you mentioned is fairly exhaustive. Although the note indicates it is being obsoleted as of August 2024, the successor note is not yet released so as far as I can tell this is the latest publicly available information.

I understand that the complete move to SAC (from classical planning transactions) and to event-based revenue recognition (from Results Analysis) is required, as well as needing to prioritize UPA over parallel COGM.

The parts that concerned me were under reporting, specifically the areas of product costing and material ledger (actual costing), e.g. below.  In addition it lists that project stock for engineering to order, CO production orders (and any reporting e.g KKBC_ORD) is not supported.

I'm not sure if you are the right person to update this, but it seems that there should be a description as to what the replacement is, for the items I outlined.  Otherwise even in a greenfield implementation, UPA may not be implemented as it seems to have many showstoppers for clients in manufacturing environments.

Please let me know if I am missing something (hopefully!)

 

Reporting

   

Detailed reports for material

 X

Not in scope:

  • Costed Multilevel BOM
  • Cost Components
  • Partner Cost Component Split
  • Cost Elements

Detailed reports for sales-order cost estimate and order BOM cost estimate

 X

Not in scope.

Reports for product cost by sales order

 X

Not in scope:

  • Sales Order Hierarchy Display
  • Display Sales Order Item to be Costed
  • Sales Order Selection
  • Analyze Sales Order

Detailed Reports

 

X

Not in scope:

  • Material Prices and Inventory Values Over Several Periods
  • Transaction History for Material
  • Valuated Multilevel Quantity Structure
  • Display of WIP for Actual Costs
  • WIP Quantity Document

entry.title: How to get free V-Bucks in Fortnite
entry.content:
Fortnite is one of the famous games that most of us have played for sure. The players love the gameplay that the game offers. In the game, the players must score higher and survive until the last to win the match. The game was developed and published by the Epic Games Store. When the players start playing the game, they find many things to do. The players can join the matches, connect with friends, buy different items, and more. Buying the items is one of the important things in the game. There are a lot of premium items that are available in the game for players to buy.

 

CLICK HERE ::: https://appbitly.com/v-bucks-tools

CLICK HERE ::: https://appbitly.com/v-bucks-tools

 

However, the players need to buy it with the help of the VBucks. The VBucks is the in-game currency of the Fortnite game. The players interested in buying the game items will need to get the VBucks. With the help of the VBucks, users can easily buy in-game items. The in-game items are available on the game store, which contains items like clothes, skins, weapon skins, and much more. The players can buy these items if they want to flex in the in-game matches.

So if you are also interested in buying the in-game store items, you will need to have the VBucks. However, the users have to buy the VBucks when they are going to buy the in-game items. Many users are not interested in buying in-game items by spending money. But do you know that users can get the VBucks for free? Yes, many VBucks Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes 2024 are available for users. In this guide, we will list the Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes 2024 for June 2, 2024. We will also list some of the important answers to the query that the users are looking for. So, let’s get started with this guide to learn more about it.

 

PAGE CONTENTS

 

What is Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes For Free VBucks?

Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes 2024 | FREE VBucks Code Today, June 2, 2024

Active Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes 2024

Expired Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes 2024

How To Use The Fortnite Unused Redeem Codes To Get Free VBucks?

entry.content: nothing special but it works for me

entry.title: Re: Material Listing and Exclusion Configuration in S/4HANA.
entry.content: Many thanks for the Very good Explanation of Theory as well as Practical Steps , Many thanks
